children.heading: Preparation; contents
children.label: a Preparation; contents
children.text: The Attorney General and the Secretary of State jointly are authorized and directed to prepare forms for the registration of aliens under , and the Attorney General is authorized and directed to prepare forms for the registration and fingerprinting of aliens under . Such forms shall contain inquiries with respect to (1) the date and place of entry of the alien into the United States; (2) activities in which he has been and intends to be engaged; (3) the length of time he expects to remain in the United States; (4) the police and criminal record, if any, of such alien; and (5) such additional matters as may be prescribed.section 1301 of this titlesection 1302 of this title

children.heading: Confidential nature
children.label: b Confidential nature
children.text: All registration and fingerprint records made under the provisions of this subchapter shall be confidential, and shall be made available only (1) pursuant to , and (2) to such persons or agencies as may be designated by the Attorney General.section 1357(f)(2) of this title

children.heading: Information under oath
children.label: c Information under oath
children.text: Every person required to apply for the registration of himself or another under this subchapter shall submit under oath the information required for such registration. Any person authorized under regulations issued by the Attorney General to register aliens under this subchapter shall be authorized to administer oaths for such purpose.

children.heading: Certificate of alien registration or alien receipt card
children.label: d Certificate of alien registration or alien receipt card
children.text: Every alien in the United States who has been registered and fingerprinted under the provisions of the Alien Registration Act, 1940, or under the provisions of this chapter shall be issued a certificate of alien registration or an alien registration receipt card in such form and manner and at such time as shall be prescribed under regulations issued by the Attorney General.

children.heading: Personal possession of registration or receipt card; penalties
children.label: e Personal possession of registration or receipt card; penalties
children.text: Every alien, eighteen years of age and over, shall at all times carry with him and have in his personal possession any certificate of alien registration or alien registration receipt card issued to him pursuant to subsection (d). Any alien who fails to comply with the provisions of this subsection shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and shall upon conviction for each offense be fined not to exceed $100 or be imprisoned not more than thirty days, or both.

children.heading: Alien’s social security account number
children.label: f Alien’s social security account number
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Attorney General is authorized to require any alien to provide the alien’s social security account number for purposes of inclusion in any record of the alien maintained by the Attorney General or the Service.
